text: Plugins for Excel, PowerPoint, Word, … coming soon https://t.co/BvT3aEO6Zk
summary: Plugins for Excel, PowerPoint, Word, … coming soon
ai_summary: Elon Musk announced that ChatGPT plugins are coming soon for Microsoft Office applications (Excel, PowerPoint, Word), signaling OpenAI's expansion into enterprise productivity software. This represents a strategic move to integrate AI capabilities directly into widely-used business tools, extending ChatGPT's reach beyond its standalone platform and potentially driving adoption across enterprises that rely on Office Suite daily.
embed_html:
Plugins for Excel, PowerPoint, Word, … coming soon https://t.co/BvT3aEO6Zk

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 20, 2026

This directly relates to xAI's product roadmap and competitive positioning in the AI race, revealing his conviction that Grok 5 represents a breakthrough toward AGI—a key milestone for his AI company's strategic direction and market differentiation.

parent.text: Scott Walter (@GoingBallistic5) explains Tesla Optimus' innovative rolling contact finger joints from the newly published patents.

parent.embed_html:
Scott Walter (@GoingBallistic5) explains Tesla Optimus' innovative rolling contact finger joints from the newly published patents. pic.twitter.com/x81qTf9cFg

— The Humanoid Hub (@TheHumanoidHub) April 19, 2026

ai_summary: Scott Walter analyzed Tesla Optimus' innovative rolling contact finger joint design from published patents, but Elon Musk clarified that Tesla has already pivoted away from this approach because the original design proved non-functional. This illustrates a critical operational lesson: published patents don't always reflect current engineering direction, and successful robotics development requires rapid iteration and design abandonment when prototypes fail—a key challenge for Tesla's humanoid robot commercialization timeline.
embed_html:
We already changed the design. This one didn’t actually work.

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 19, 2026

Ford CEO Jim Farley in new interview on Chinese EV competition:

"If you’re an American and you want us to beat the Chinese in the car business, you’re all going to want to pay attention, not necessarily to @Tesla. Nothing against Tesla—they’ve been doing great—but they really… pic.twitter.com/ratm91CxoL

— Sawyer Merritt (@SawyerMerritt) April 19, 2026

ai_summary: Ford's CEO publicly cautioned Americans about Chinese EV competition, prompting Elon Musk to clarify that Tesla's competitive advantage in China will significantly increase once supervised Full Self-Driving (FSD) is approved there, with production capacity at Tesla's Shanghai factory being the primary constraint—not technology maturity. This reveals Tesla's strategy to leverage autonomous driving capabilities and manufacturing scale as differentiators against Chinese EV makers in the critical Chinese market.
embed_html:
This is before supervised FSD is approved in China. Limiting factor is production output in Shanghai.

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 19, 2026
